indexes.name: adsh_dict
indexes.layout: uint32_t N, then N entries of {char[20] adsh, int32_t code} sorted by adsh
indexes.usage: query startup: translate adsh string filter → int32_t code; O(log N) binary search

columns.name: adsh_code
columns.encoding: adsh dict index (0..86134); direct array index into sub columns

columns.name: tagver_code
columns.encoding: tag table row index (0..1070661); enables O(1) join to tag and pre

columns.name: uom_code
columns.encoding: uom dict index (0..29); USD=code loaded from uom_codes.bin at query startup

columns.name: value
columns.encoding: raw IEEE 754 double, NaN for NULL

indexes.name: num_zone_maps
indexes.layout: uint32_t N_blocks; then N_blocks × {int8_t min_uom, int8_t max_uom, int32_t min_ddate, int32_t max_ddate} (10 bytes each)
indexes.usage: Q2: skip blocks where [min_uom,max_uom] excludes pure_code; Q24: skip blocks where [min_uom,max_uom] excludes usd_code OR [min_ddate,max_ddate] excludes [20230101,20231231]

indexes.name: uom_codes
indexes.layout: uint8_t N_uom; then N_uom × {int8_t code, char[24] uom_string}
indexes.usage: query startup: load 'USD','pure','shares' → int8_t code; used in zone map and row-level filter

columns.name: tag_str
columns.encoding: offset+data; row i = tag string for tagver_code i

columns.name: version_str
columns.encoding: offset+data; row i = version string for tagver_code i

indexes.name: tagver_dict
indexes.layout: uint32_t N; then N entries of {int32_t code, uint16_t tag_len, uint16_t ver_len, char tag_bytes[], char ver_bytes[]} sorted by (tag,version)
indexes.usage: ingest-time only: translate (tag_str,ver_str) → tagver_code for num/pre; not needed at query time since no tag/version filter in workload

indexes.name: pre_adsh_tagver_set
indexes.layout: uint64_t N_unique; then N_unique uint64_t keys = ((uint64_t)adsh_code<<32)|tagver_code, sorted ascending
indexes.usage: Q24 anti-join: load into unordered_set<uint64_t>; for each num row check membership; Q4/Q6: scan pre.stmt_code, collect matching adsh_code+tagver_code, build hash map, probe with num rows

indexes.name: stmt_codes
indexes.layout: uint8_t N_stmt; then N_stmt × {int8_t code, char[4] stmt_string}
indexes.usage: query startup: load 'EQ','IS','BS','CF' → int8_t code for filter comparison

date_encoding: YYYYMMDD_as_int32 (no epoch conversion; range predicates work directly on integer comparison)
varlen_string_format: uint32_t offsets[N+1] in <col>_offsets.bin; char data[] in <col>_data.bin; string_i = data[offsets[i]..offsets[i+1]]

hardware_config.notes: HDD I/O is bottleneck for large files; parallel table parsing limited to independent tables; in-memory sort safe for 39M rows (945MB); 376GB RAM allows full dataset in memory during ingest

encoding_rationale.adsh_code: 86135 unique adsh strings; dict-encoded to int32_t enables O(1) sub lookup (sub row_i = adsh_code_i) and compact join keys; saves ~16 bytes/row in num (39M rows = 629MB savings)
encoding_rationale.tagver_code: 1.07M unique (tag,version) pairs; single int32_t encodes composite FK; enables direct tag-table row access without hash lookup at query time; saves ~276 bytes/row in num and pre
encoding_rationale.uom_code: 30 distinct values; int8_t vs 20-byte string saves 19 bytes/row × 39M = 741MB; zone maps on sorted-by-uom num enable block skipping for pure/USD filters
encoding_rationale.stmt_code: 7 distinct values; int8_t filter in Q1,Q4,Q6; sequential scan of stmt_code.bin (9.6MB) is fast even on HDD
encoding_rationale.sort_num_by_uom_ddate: Groups all USD rows (87%) in first 34M rows; pure rows (0.24%) in ~95K contiguous rows; zone maps skip 99.76% of blocks for Q2 pure filter; combined uom+ddate zone maps skip non-2023 USD blocks for Q24
encoding_rationale.sort_pre_by_adsh_tagver: Enables O(N log N) construction of pre hash set; sequential read for building join structures; pre_adsh_tagver_set.bin built once, used by Q4/Q6/Q24 for anti-join and hash join setup
